channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B

item.title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice
item.dc:creator: Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Benchmarking the effective many-body flat-band projected model of magic-angle bilayer graphene against the full tight-binding description
item.description:
Author(s): Miguel Sánchez Sánchez and Tobias Stauber

We benchmark the recently proposed projection method [M. Sánchez Sánchez et al., Phys. Rev. B 111, 205133 (2025)] for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (MATBG) across various symmetry-breaking phases at charge neutrality. The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035155] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Miguel Sánchez Sánchez and Tobias Stauber

We benchmark the recently proposed projection method [M. Sánchez Sánchez et al., Phys. Rev. B 111, 205133 (2025)] for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (MATBG) across various symmetry-breaking phases at charge neutrality. The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035155] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Benchmarking the effective many-body flat-band projected model of magic-angle bilayer graphene against the full tight-binding description

item.title: Metal-insulator transition in a boundary three chain model
item.description:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.title: Probing quantum geometric nonlinear magnetization via second-harmonic magneto-optical Kerr effect
item.description:
Author(s): Xuan Qian, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Wenkai Zhu, Yuqing Huang, Yiyuan Chen, Hai-Zhou Lu, Yang Ji, and Kaiyou Wang

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xuan Qian, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Wenkai Zhu, Yuqing Huang, Yiyuan Chen, Hai-Zhou Lu, Yang Ji, and Kaiyou Wang

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Magnetic injection photocurrents in valley-polarized states of twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Fernando Peñaranda, Héctor Ochoa, and Fernando de Juan

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene displays a complex phase diagram as a function of flat-band filling, featuring compressibility cascade transitions and a variety of competing ground states with broken spin, valley, and point-group symmetries. Recent terahertz photocurrent spectroscopy experiment…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035443] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fernando Peñaranda, Héctor Ochoa, and Fernando de Juan

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene displays a complex phase diagram as a function of flat-band filling, featuring compressibility cascade transitions and a variety of competing ground states with broken spin, valley, and point-group symmetries. Recent terahertz photocurrent spectroscopy experiment…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035443] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic injection photocurrents in valley-polarized states of twisted bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Fernando Peñaranda, Héctor Ochoa, and Fernando de Juan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable anisotropy from twisted rhombic homobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao

The authors demonstrate here that twisted semiconductor homobilayers with band extrema at the Y valley on diamond lattices provide a promising platform for realizing a single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable hopping anisotropy via a displacement field. In the absence of a displacement field, the low-energy physics is governed by weakly coupled chains with quasi-one-dimensional band structure; increasing the displacement field generates interchain hopping, realizing a two-dimensional triangular lattice with tunable hopping anisotropy. To gain further insight into the role of hopping anisotropy, they employ density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) to explore the many-body physics of this model at U/t = 10 and half filling. The resulting phase diagram includes chiral spin liquid, nonmagnetic, and Néel antiferromagnetic phases, all accessible through displacement field control.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041119] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao

The authors demonstrate here that twisted semiconductor homobilayers with band extrema at the Y valley on diamond lattices provide a promising platform for realizing a single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable hopping anisotropy via a displacement field. In the absence of a displacement field, the low-energy physics is governed by weakly coupled chains with quasi-one-dimensional band structure; increasing the displacement field generates interchain hopping, realizing a two-dimensional triangular lattice with tunable hopping anisotropy. To gain further insight into the role of hopping anisotropy, they employ density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) to explore the many-body physics of this model at U/t = 10 and half filling. The resulting phase diagram includes chiral spin liquid, nonmagnetic, and Néel antiferromagnetic phases, all accessible through displacement field control.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041119] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable anisotropy from twisted rhombic homobilayers
item.dc:creator: Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Symmetry-breaking perturbations in the Jahn-Teller-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Natsuki Okada, Tatsuya Miki, Yusuke Nomura, Philipp Werner, and Shintaro Hoshino

We study the effect of symmetry-breaking perturbations in the multiorbital Hubbard model coupled to anisotropic Jahn-Teller phonons, which is relevant for the description of fulleride superconductors. This system is often approximated by a model with static antiferromagnetic (AFM) Hund's coupling, i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024517] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Natsuki Okada, Tatsuya Miki, Yusuke Nomura, Philipp Werner, and Shintaro Hoshino

We study the effect of symmetry-breaking perturbations in the multiorbital Hubbard model coupled to anisotropic Jahn-Teller phonons, which is relevant for the description of fulleride superconductors. This system is often approximated by a model with static antiferromagnetic (AFM) Hund's coupling, i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024517] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-breaking perturbations in the Jahn-Teller-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Natsuki Okada, Tatsuya Miki, Yusuke Nomura, Philipp Werner, and Shintaro Hoshino

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Electronic band structure of one-dimensional moiré chains
item.description:
Author(s): Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky

We consider a general model of two atomic chains forming a moiré pattern due to a small mismatch in their lattice spacings, given by $θ=({a}_{1}−{a}_{2})/{a}_{2}$. Assuming arbitrary single-band dispersion relations ${ɛ}_{1}(p)$ and ${ɛ}_{2}(q)$ for the chains, along with an arbitrary interchain cou…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035154] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky

We consider a general model of two atomic chains forming a moiré pattern due to a small mismatch in their lattice spacings, given by θ=(a1−a2)/a2. Assuming arbitrary single-band dispersion relations ɛ1(p) and ɛ2(q) for the chains, along with an arbitrary interchain coupling term T(x), we show that t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035154] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic band structure of one-dimensional moiré chains
item.dc:creator: Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky

item.title: Raman scattering from moiré phonons
item.description:
Author(s): Vitor Dantas, Héctor Ochoa, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Natalia B. Perkins

We develop a theoretical framework for probing moiré phonon modes using Raman spectroscopy, and illustrate it with the example of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG). These moiré phonons arise from interlayer sliding motion in twisted two-dimensional materials and correspond to fluctuations of the stacki…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041117] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

Author(s): Vitor Dantas, Héctor Ochoa, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Natalia B. Perkins

We develop a theoretical framework for probing moiré phonon modes using Raman spectroscopy, and illustrate it with the example of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG). These moiré phonons arise from interlayer sliding motion in twisted two-dimensional materials and correspond to fluctuations of the stacki…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041117] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.title: Spin-Seebeck signatures of spin chirality in kagome antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Seebeck signatures of spin chirality in kagome antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Elementary building blocks for cluster Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Two-site entanglement in the two-dimensional Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Spin susceptibility in a pseudogap state with fluctuating spiral magnetic order
item.description:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Quantum geometry from the Moyal product: Quantum kinetic equation and nonlinear response
item.description:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with $U$(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with U(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals
item.description:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}\phantom{\rule{4pt}{0ex}}[\mathrm{Cs}{({\mathrm{V}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on CsV3Sb5 doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped CsV3Sb5[Cs(V1−xCrx)3Sb5] exhibits a simultaneous weakening of the CDW and superconductivity as the doping concentrat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals

item.title: Topological phase transitions and quarter-metal states of rhombohedral trilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-Sen Jin, Xi-Yue Wang, and Hang Xie

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-Sen Jin, Xi-Yue Wang, and Hang Xie

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological phase transitions and quarter-metal states of rhombohedral trilayer graphene

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.dc:creator: Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.33}{\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of Cu0.33TiSe2 across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in the normal state above the CD…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy

item.title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.description:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta $\mathbf{k}$ and $−\mathbf{k}$. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multipl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta k and −k. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multiple metallic, and Mott-i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.dc:creator: Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
